title: Softly Roasted Polish Potato Casserole
description: A hearty, pillowy Polish dish featuring roasted potatoes, perfect for a post-workout meal. This gluten-free friendly casserole is made with vegetable oil and mushimono-steam.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the sliced potatoes with 2 tbsp of vegetable oil, salt, black pepper, and paprika until they are evenly coated.
Spread the potatoes in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes.
In a large skillet, heat the remaining 2 tbsp of vegetable oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until it is softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the sliced mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they are tender, about 5 minutes.
In a large casserole dish, create a layer of roasted potatoes.
Add a layer of the mushroom and onion mixture on top of the potatoes.
Repeat the layers until all of the ingredients have been used, ending with a layer of potatoes on top.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and steam in the oven for 10 minutes using the mushimono-steam method.
